pick.rationale: Arkansas holds a narrow but consistent edge over Wisconsin in every major efficiency metric — KenPom #17 (AdjEM +25.43) vs. #22 (+23.4), Torvik #20 vs. #21 — anchored by the 5th-best offense in the country (AdjOE 127.6) that gives the Razorbacks a slight scoring advantage over a Wisconsin offense ranked 11th nationally. Both teams enter near full strength: Darius Acuff Jr. (SEC Player of the Year) returned from his ankle injury for the SEC Tournament and led Arkansas to the championship, while Nolan Winter confirmed his return from an ankle injury for Wisconsin after missing the Big Ten Tournament. Arkansas's SEC Tournament title run, superior offensive firepower, and the marginal efficiency edge across all major rating systems make them the narrow right side in what is otherwise one of the most evenly matched games in the bracket, though Wisconsin's Big Ten battle-testing and full-strength roster give them a genuine and credible path to an upset.

reasoningStep.title: Wisconsin vs. Arkansas — Round of 32 West Region Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Arkansas holds a slim but consistent efficiency edge (KenPom #17 vs. #22, ~2-point AdjEM gap) over Wisconsin in a near-coin-flip 4/5 matchup. Both teams return to full health — Acuff for Arkansas, Winter for Wisconsin. Arkansas's elite offense (5th nationally) and SEC Tournament championship form tip the balance narrowly toward the Razorbacks.
evidence: Arkansas KenPom #17, AdjEM +25.43, AdjOE 127.6 (5th nationally), AdjDE 102.1 (50th); Torvik #20
Wisconsin KenPom #22, AdjEM +23.4, AdjOE 125.4 (11th nationally), AdjDE 102.0 (48th); Torvik #21
Darius Acuff Jr. (SEC Player of the Year, ankle) confirmed available — played in SEC Tournament, won championship
Nolan Winter (ankle) confirmed return for NCAA Tournament after missing Big Ten Tournament; Jack Janicki (wrist) also returning
Arkansas is 4-seed, Wisconsin is 5-seed — classic near-coin-flip matchup with Arkansas holding slight edge in all metrics
Arkansas won SEC Tournament championship; Wisconsin lost Big Ten semifinal to Michigan 68-65 without Winter
Both teams carry elite offensive profiles with nearly identical defensive rankings (~48th-50th nationally)

toolCalls.summary: Fetched https://badgerswire.usatoday.com/story/sports/college/badgers/mens-basketball/2026/03/17/wisconsin-nolan-winter-injury-update/89192193007/ and extracted 2019 characters of text.

result.text: Wisconsin basketball Nolan Winter injury update NCAA Tournament Skip to main content Home Schedule Football Basketball Recruiting Big Ten Sports Odds Newsletter College Sports Wire USA TODAY Sports NCAA Tournament status update for Wisconsin forward Nolan Winter Jordon Lawrenz Badgers Wire Ahead of the 2025-26 NCAA Tournament, Wisconsin Badgers star Nolan Winter has provided an update that has fans on the edge of their seats. Winter missed the entirety of the Big Ten Tournament, which may have impacted Wisconsin's last-second loss to Michigan in the semifinals. The team certainly got some help from Austin Rapp, but at the end of the day, it fell three points shy of advancing to the championship game. Had Winter played in that matchup, things could've gone entirely differently. Wisconsin had to rely on the three-ball a lot more than usual, though that certainly has been its bread and butter with Nick Boyd and John Blackwell leading the way. Regardless, Winter took to Instagram on Monday and posted "go time" with six different images. Head coach Greg Gard has long said the junior will be back in time for March Madness, and it seems like Winter himself has now confirmed that. Wisconsin still ended up being a No. 5 seed in the NCAA Tournament, which is quite impressive. It is stuck with a tough first-round matchup, but Winter's size and presence are enough to change the game on a dime. The Minnesota native last played on March 4 against Maryland, where he finished with seven points and six rebounds. He's a walking double-double machine and joins Boyd and Blackwell as the only players on this squad who average 10+ points per game. modelTrace.content:
I now have sufficient data to make a well-informed pick. Let me synthesize everything:

**Key findings:**

**Ratings (extremely close):**
- Arkansas: KenPom #17, AdjEM +25.43, AdjOE 127.6 (5th), AdjDE 102.1 (50th) — 4-seed
- Wisconsin: KenPom #22, AdjEM +23.4, AdjOE 125.4 (11th), AdjDE 102.0 (48th) — 5-seed
- Arkansas holds a ~2-point efficiency edge; Torvik is nearly identical (#20 vs. #21)

**Seeds:** Arkansas is the 4-seed, Wisconsin the 5-seed — a classic 4/5 matchup

**Injuries:**
- Wisconsin: Nolan Winter (ankle) missed the Big Ten Tournament but confirmed return for NCAA Tournament ("go time" Instagram post); Jack Janicki (wrist) also returning — Wisconsin near full strength
- Arkansas: Darius Acuff Jr. (SEC Player of the Year, ankle) was rested for regular-season finale but played in SEC Tournament and won the title — Arkansas at full strength

**Schedule/Form:**
- Arkansas: 25-8 (per ratings), SEC Tournament champions, elite offense (5th nationally), SEC battle-tested under John Calipari
- Wisconsin: 24-10, Big Ten runner-up (lost to Michigan 68-65 in semis without Winter), Big Ten battle-tested under Greg Gard

**Matchup context:**
- Both teams have nearly identical defensive profiles (~48th-50th nationally)
- Arkansas's offense is slightly superior (5th vs. 11th nationally)
- Arkansas has the slight efficiency edge and the higher seed
- The SI West Region article specifically noted Arkansas as the SEC Tournament winner in the West

**Upset path for Wisconsin:** Credible — they're essentially the same caliber team, Big Ten battle-tested, and returning to full health. But Arkansas's superior offense and slight efficiency edge, combined with their SEC Tournament championship form, give them the narrow edge.

```json
{
  "pick": {
    "gameId": "r32-west-2",
    "winnerId": "arkansas",
    "confidence": 0.57,
    "rationale": "Arkansas holds a narrow but consistent edge over Wisconsin in every major efficiency metric — KenPom #17 (AdjEM +25.43) vs. #22 (+23.4), Torvik #20 vs. #21 — anchored by the 5th-best offense in the country (AdjOE 127.6) that gives the Razorbacks a slight scoring advantage over a Wisconsin offense ranked 11th nationally. Both teams enter near full strength: Darius Acuff Jr. (SEC Player of the Year) returned from his ankle injury for the SEC Tournament and led Arkansas to the championship, while Nolan Winter confirmed his return from an ankle injury for Wisconsin after missing the Big Ten Tournament. Arkansas's SEC Tournament title run, superior offensive firepower, and the marginal efficiency edge across all major rating systems make them the narrow right side in what is otherwise one of the most evenly matched games in the bracket, though Wisconsin's Big Ten battle-testing and full-strength roster give them a genuine and credible path to an upset."
  },
```
